name: Gemma 4
archDisplay: Dense Transformer, hybrid local/global attention (31B flagship variant)
note: Most capable open Gemma yet; 31B dense flagship ranks #3 among open models on the Arena AI text leaderboard.
tpsNote: Third-party RTX 4090 tok/s figures found in the wild referenced non-existent Gemma 4 sizes (7B/9B/27B) and were discarded as unreliable.

results.notes: LiveCodeBench v6, 31B dense, also confirmed on huggingface.co/google/gemma-4-31B-it.
Codeforces Elo, 31B dense (26B A4B sibling scores 1718 on the same table, already in corpus).
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools, 31B. Card also lists HLE-with-search 26.5, a distinct tool-augmented setting omitted to avoid conflating with the single 'hle' slug.
Multilingual MMLU, 31B. Slightly above the taxonomy's typical 30-88 range ceiling but confirmed identically on two independent primary sources (ai.google.dev and huggingface.co).

Launch-time LMArena (Arena AI) text-leaderboard Elo for the 31B dense Gemma 4 model: 1452, ranked #3 overall at launch, above much larger open models such as GPT-OSS-120B. This exact figure is already cited in the record's own verificationNotes prose but was never added as a formal results-array entry. Note the live leaderboard score drifts over time as new models are added (independently observed at ~1486 on arena.ai as of mid-July 2026), so this is the well-documented launch snapshot rather than a live figure.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 25/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Gemma 4 31B" (Google). SealedBench 'Gemma 4 31B' is this corpus's 'Gemma 4' (31B dense flagship).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: released 2026-04-02 (blog.google + deepmind.google, corroborated by independent press). Model card (ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4, fetched directly) confirms the 31B variant is DENSE with hybrid local/global sliding-window attention (not MoE — a separate 26B A4B MoE variant exists in the family, which is what some press coverage of 'Gemma 4 MoE' refers to), 30.7B total params, 256K context, Apache-2.0 license, and all 3 benchmark numbers (MMLU-Pro 85.2, AIME 2026 89.2, GPQA Diamond 84.3) literally confirmed on that page. hf link google/gemma-4-31B-it confirmed to exist. Arena #3 ranking (1452 Elo) independently corroborated by arena.ai. No changes to record.

2026-07-16: Researched the 5 missing core slugs (mmlu, math, humaneval, swe-bench, mmmu) across all 3 required source types; none is published for Gemma 4 31B, and this is judged a genuine absence rather than a gap in research. Official sources -- ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4 (fetched directly), huggingface.co/google/gemma-4-31B-it's README benchmark table, and the Gemma 4 Technical Report (arxiv.org/abs/2607.02770, Table 5, fetched via the arxiv.org/html/2607.02770 HTML mirror) -- all report only the harder/successor variants MMLU-Pro (85.2), MATH-Vision (85.6), and MMMU-Pro (76.9), with no HumanEval or SWE-bench row anywhere in any of the three. The sibling gemma-4-26b-a4b.json record, drawn from the identical shared benchmark table, shows the exact same substitution pattern (MMLU-Pro/MATH-Vision/MMMU-Pro, no plain MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/SWE-bench), corroborating this is a deliberate family-wide publishing choice, not an oversight specific to this file. Independent leaderboards vals.ai/models/google_gemma-4-31b-it and artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemma-4-31b were checked directly and neither tracks MMLU, MATH, HumanEval, SWE-bench, or MMMU for this model either (Vals.ai lists only MortgageTax and SAGE; Artificial Analysis lists GDPval-AA v2, Tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, and MMMU-Pro). Several low-quality SEO/content-farm sites (gemma4.wiki, gemma4-ai.com, benchlm.ai, tech-insider.org) surfaced fabricated-looking numbers for these slugs in search snippets -- e.g. an 'HumanEval pass@1 80' claim suspiciously identical to the already-verified LiveCodeBench score, and a SWE-Rebench (a distinct benchmark) 41.6% figure mislabeled in one snippet as SWE-bench -- but none of these is a primary or independently-audited source, none was corroborated by any credible source, and they were disregarded rather than used. Consistent with the pattern noted elsewhere in this corpus, frontier/open-weight labs increasingly skip saturated legacy benchmarks (plain MMLU, MATH, HumanEval) in favor of harder Pro/Vision variants, and Google appears not to have run or published a standalone SWE-bench or plain-MMMU evaluation for this family; all 5 gaps are therefore omitted rather than guessed.

2026-07-18: Re-chased the swe-bench and mmmu gaps specifically (round-22 pass), per instruction to treat both as current-generation benchmarks worth extra search effort. Confirmed via corpus file that this model IS vision-capable (modalities: [text, vision], capabilities.vision: true) -- so mmmu is not architecturally inapplicable, it would have to be a genuine reporting gap if absent.

SWE-bench (Verified): Re-checked ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4 and huggingface.co/google/gemma-4-31B-it directly (WebFetch) -- neither lists SWE-bench in any form. Checked artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemma-4-31b (WebFetch) -- its full tracked-benchmark list (GDPval-AA v2, Tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, IFBench, APEX-Agents-AA, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro) contains no SWE-bench entry. Checked vals.ai/models/google_gemma-4-31b-it -- only MortgageTax and SAGE are listed, no SWE-bench. Checked llm-stats.com/models/gemma-4-31b-it -- benchmark charts are JS-rendered/empty in fetched content, no static SWE-bench figure available. Checked the arxiv.org/abs/2607.02770 abstract page (technical report) -- no benchmark table in the abstract view; Table 5 was already fetched via the HTML mirror in the prior (2026-07-16) pass and confirmed to contain no SWE-bench row. Web search surfaced two numbers purporting to be Gemma-4-family SWE-bench results, both rejected: (1) a 'SWE-Rebench... 41.6%' figure for 'Gemma 4 31B', which is a distinct benchmark (SWE-Rebench, not SWE-bench) mislabeled by low-quality SEO sites -- this exact conflation was already caught and excluded in the 2026-07-16 pass; (2) a Medium/TowardsAI article (pub.towardsai.net, fetched directly) stating 'Gemma 4 26B A4B scores 52.0% on SWE-bench Verified' -- this is for the 26B A4B MoE sibling variant, not the 31B dense flagship this record covers, and is a single non-primary blog source with no methodology/harness disclosed, so even if taken at face value it would not be attributable to this record. No credible primary or independently-audited source publishes a SWE-bench Verified score for gemma-4-31B-it. Confirmed absent, not just unfound.

MMMU (plain, distinct from MMMU-Pro already in corpus at 76.9): Re-checked the same primary sources (model card, HF README, Artificial Analysis, Vals.ai, llm-stats.com) -- all either omit MMMU entirely or list only MMMU-Pro. Web search surfaced a '78.1% vs Qwen 3.6's 76.5%' MMMU figure; traced to qwen3lm.com/qwen3.6-gemma4/ (fetched directly), which attributes it to 'Gemma 4 70B' -- a model size that does not exist anywhere in the real Gemma 4 lineup (flagship is the 30.7B/31B dense model in this record; the only other released size is 26B A4B MoE). This mirrors the previously-documented pattern of SEO content-farm sites inventing benchmark numbers against fictitious Gemma 4 variants (cf. the already-flagged bogus 7B/9B/27B tok/s figures) and is disregarded as fabricated. ai.rs's comparison table was also checked directly and confirms only MMMU-Pro (76.9%), no separate plain-MMMU figure, with no source citation for any of its numbers. No credible source reports a distinct plain-MMMU score for gemma-4-31B-it -- Google appears to report only the harder MMMU-Pro variant for this model, consistent with the family-wide 'Pro/Vision variant instead of legacy benchmark' substitution pattern already documented for mmlu/math/humaneval. Both gaps remain omitted_no_evidence after this deeper pass; no new results-array entries added.
